name: California Online Privacy Protection Act
description: California law (effective 2004-07-01; amended 2014 to address Do Not Track) requiring operators of commercial websites and online services that collect personally identifiable information from California consumers to conspicuously post a privacy policy. The 2014 amendment (AB 370) requires the policy to describe how the operator responds to Do Not Track signals. Codified at California Business and Professions Code §§22575-22579.
relationToCloudSecurity: Any cloud-hosted website or service collecting California consumer PII must satisfy CalOPPA's privacy-policy posting requirements.
relationToAISecurity: AI services collecting California user data must satisfy CalOPPA notice obligations. Predates and is largely overshadowed by CCPA/CPRA but remains in force.

license.notes: US state statutes are public-domain government works and may be freely redistributed.

lifecycle.notes: Effective 2004-07-01. Amended 2014 (AB 370) for Do Not Track.

current_extraction.notes: Acquired from leginfo.legislature.ca.gov as HTML, converted to markdown. California Business and Professions Code Sections 22575-22579 (Internet Privacy Requirements). The first ~60 lines of the markdown are legislative navigation chrome; actual law text begins at the '22575.' anchor. _source.html kept locally as gitignored artifact.

notes: California privacy-policy mandate. Older but still operative; acquire from leginfo for structured extraction.
